response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and systems software developer, but his experience is almost entirely in web, mobile, and backend product engineering. The role demands SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design at a principal level — none of which appear anywhere in his history. His low-latency DLang work and brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but they are far from the silicon-level performance architecture this position requires.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (DLang async I/O services handling millions of requests per second)
Broad, 18+ year engineering career spanning embedded, desktop, web, and mobile with solid craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some C/C++ experience on engineering tooling (NI LabWindows/CVI) and audio DSP thesis work
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator performance background
Career trajectory is web/product full-stack, not principal-level performance architecture; recent work (TypeScript, React, MongoDB) is unrelated to the role

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
